summary: A single Jest test assertion failed in `test/config/get.ts`. The test `pnpm config get reads npm options but ignores other settings from .npmrc` expected the registry URL `"https://my-org.registry.example.com"` (without trailing slash) but received `"https://my-org.registry.example.com/"` (with trailing slash), causing the entire CI job to exit with code 1.
root_cause: The `pnpm config get` command returns a registry URL with a trailing slash (`https://my-org.registry.example.com/`) but the test asserts it should equal `https://my-org.registry.example.com` (no trailing slash). This `toBe` equality check fails because the strings differ by one character.
relevant_tests: pnpm config get reads npm options but ignores other settings from .npmrc

evidence.reason: Identifies the specific test file that failed.

evidence.quote: ● pnpm config get reads npm options but ignores other settings from .npmrc
evidence.reason: Identifies the exact test case that failed.
Shows the exact assertion mismatch: expected no trailing slash, received a trailing slash.
Shows the test assertion at line 32 of test/config/get.ts that failed.

evidence.quote:
Test Suites: 1 failed, 3 skipped, 61 passed, 62 of 65 total
Tests:       1 failed, 9 skipped, 475 passed, 485 total
evidence.reason: Confirms exactly one test failed across the entire test run.
Confirms the jest run exited with a non-zero code, failing the CI job.

suggested_fix: Either update the test assertion in `test/config/get.ts:32` to accept a trailing slash (`'https://my-org.registry.example.com/'`), or fix the `pnpm config get` implementation to strip the trailing slash from registry URLs before returning them.
